old_claim.note: Plant Domestication provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim_absent: No plant-domestication dependency remains because microscopy identifies the woven fibers as locally gathered tree bast rather than domesticated flax.

source_shape.source_locator: Abstract reports scanning-electron-microscope identification of locally sourced oak bast and questions prior assumptions about domesticated flax fiber.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The Catalhoyuk woven textiles use local wild tree-bast fibers rather than a domesticated fiber crop.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: Gathered bast can support the evidenced weaving directly, so crop domestication is not a necessary enabling stage for this node.

ontology_before: All early woven textiles were assumed to require domesticated plant fiber.
ontology_after: The Catalhoyuk node reflects direct evidence for local wild tree-bast weaving.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed: textiles_weaving no longer directly depends on plant_domestication.
would_reject_if: The analyzed Catalhoyuk fibers were reidentified as domesticated flax or another cultivated fiber crop.
short_reason: The direct fiber analysis identifies local tree bast, not a domesticated crop.
